title: Percutaneous Balloon Mitral Valvuloplasty Protocol
author: Soad M. Hegazy

steps.instruction: adult patients from both genders undergoing percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty procedure were recruited from the above mentioned settings . The study tools were:

steps.title: Patients` health needs assessment sheet (pre/post...
steps.instruction: Patients` health needs assessment sheet (pre/post procedure) that included physical , psychological , social , spiritual and educational needs .

steps.title: Anxiety Rating Scale (pre/post procedure) to assess anxiety level
steps.instruction: Anxiety Rating Scale (pre/post procedure) to assess anxiety level .

steps.title: Numerical pain scale (pre/post procedure) to assess pain severity level
steps.instruction: Numerical pain scale (pre/post procedure) to assess pain severity level . Results : More than half of the studied patients were working , with age above 35 years , male and married. In addition , they had health needs pre procedure . Conclusion : The present study concluded that patients under the study had higher needs : physical, psychological, social, spiritual and educational pre percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty (PBMV) procedure compared to post procedure . In addition, the highest needs pre procedure were physical followed by educational, spiritual, psychological and then later social . Recommendations: Further studies should be carried out on a large number of such group of patients with evidence of results and generalization .    Keywords:  Percutaneous Balloon Mitral Valvuloplasty - Patients`Health Needs Management - Proposed Protocol.    Title:  Percutaneous Balloon Mitral Valvuloplasty : Proposed Protocol for Patients Health Needs Management   Author:  Soad M. Hegazy, Samiyah M. Aljohani, Hend M. Omar, Mohammed M. Abualruhaylah, Saleh M. Mayyas. description: Abstract:  Patient undergoing procedure of percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty (PBMV) have many needs that should be assessed to improve both the quality and value of care . Aim: This study aimed to propose a protocol for patients` health needs management during percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty (PBMV ) procedure .
